title: Preliminary 2022/23 Grain Trade Forecast
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) recently released their first global grain export forecast for the upcoming 2022/23 season. The forecast is still very preliminary, but of note is that only 487.3 million tons of exports are expected. If 487.3 million tons is ultimately exported, then this would mark a year-on-year decline of 12.9 million tons (-3%) from the 500.2 million tons that is expected for the current 2021/22 season.
